content:
Patient: John Doe
Date: 2023-10-15
Doctor: Dr. Priya Sharma

ANNUAL PHYSICAL EXAM REPORT

Patient presents for a routine annual physical exam. Patient reports feeling well with no major complaints.

Vitals:
- Blood Pressure: 122/78 mmHg
- Heart Rate: 70 bpm
- Temperature: 98.6°F (37°C)
- Height: 5'10" (178 cm)
- Weight: 175 lbs (79.4 kg)
- BMI: 25.1

Physical Examination:
- GENERAL: Alert, oriented, in no acute distress.
- EYES: PERRLA. No icterus.
- ENT: Mucous membranes moist. No oropharyngeal erythema.
- CARDIOVASCULAR: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops.
- RESPIRATORY: Lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally.
- ABDOMEN: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Bowel sounds normal.

Assessment & Plan:
1.  Overall healthy adult male.
2.  BMI is slightly overweight. Discussed diet and exercise. Recommended 30 minutes of moderate activity 3-5 times per week.
3.  Routine blood work ordered (see separate lab results).
4.  Follow-up in 1 year or as needed.

Electronically Signed,
Dr. Priya Sharma

Patient: John Doe
Date of Collection: 2023-10-20
Ordering Physician: Dr. Priya Sharma

BLOOD TEST RESULTS - COMPLETE BLOOD COUNT (CBC) & LIPID PANEL

--- COMPLETE BLOOD COUNT ---
- White Blood Cell Count (WBC): 6.5 x10^9/L (Normal: 4.5-11.0)
- Red Blood Cell Count (RBC): 5.1 x10^12/L (Normal: 4.7-6.1)
- Hemoglobin (Hgb): 15.2 g/dL (Normal: 14-18)
- Hematocrit (Hct): 45% (Normal: 42-52%)
- Platelet Count: 250 x10^9/L (Normal: 150-450)

--- LIPID PANEL ---
- Total Cholesterol: 195 mg/dL (Desirable: <200)
- HDL Cholesterol: 55 mg/dL (Desirable: >40)
- LDL Cholesterol: 120 mg/dL (Desirable: <130)
- Triglycerides: 100 mg/dL (Desirable: <150)

Interpretation:
- CBC is within normal limits.
- Lipid panel is within desirable ranges. No signs of dyslipidemia.

Reviewed by,
Dr. Priya Sharma

Patient: John Doe
Date: 2023-11-05
Consulting Physician: Dr. Priya Sharma

CARDIOLOGY CONSULTATION NOTE

REASON FOR CONSULTATION: Patient referred for evaluation of occasional heart palpitations reported during annual physical.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS: Patient is a 45-year-old male who reports intermittent episodes of a 'fluttering' sensation in his chest. Episodes are brief, lasting only a few seconds, and occur randomly, perhaps 1-2 times a month. No associated chest pain, shortness of breath, dizziness, or syncope.

ELECTROCARDIOGRAM (ECG): 
- Rate: 72 bpm
- Rhythm: Normal Sinus Rhythm
- Axis: Normal
- Intervals: Normal
- No ST elevation/depression, no significant Q waves.

ASSESSMENT:
Benign palpitations. The clinical picture and normal ECG are reassuring. The patient's symptoms are not suggestive of a dangerous arrhythmia at this time.

PLAN:
1. Reassurance provided to the patient about the benign nature of his symptoms.
2. Advised to reduce caffeine intake and monitor symptoms.
3. Patient to keep a log of palpitation episodes, noting any triggers.
4. Will consider a 24-hour Holter monitor if symptoms increase in frequency or change in character.
5. Follow-up in 6 months or sooner if symptoms worsen.

Electronically Signed,
Dr. Priya Sharma, Cardiologist

Patient: John Doe
Date: 2024-01-22
Prescribing Doctor: Dr. Rohan Mehra

PRESCRIPTION

Medication: Amoxicillin 500 mg

Dosage: Take one (1) capsule by mouth three (3) times a day.

Duration: For 7 days.

Quantity: 21 capsules

Refills: 0

Diagnosis: Acute Bacterial Sinusitis

Instructions:
- Take with or without food.
- Complete the entire course of antibiotics, even if you start feeling better.
- If you experience a rash, hives, or difficulty breathing, stop taking the medication and seek immediate medical attention.

Electronically Signed,
Dr. Rohan Mehra

Patient: John Doe
Date: 2024-02-10
Doctor: Dr. Rohan Mehra

FOLLOW-UP VISIT NOTE - NEUROLOGY

SUBJECTIVE: Patient returns for follow-up regarding treatment for acute sinusitis. He reports that he completed the full 7-day course of Amoxicillin as prescribed. He notes significant improvement in his symptoms, including resolution of facial pressure and nasal congestion. He denies any ongoing headaches or fever.

OBJECTIVE:
- Vitals: Stable.
- Physical Exam: Patient appears well. No sinus tenderness on palpation.

ASSESSMENT:
- Resolution of acute bacterial sinusitis.

PLAN:
- No further antibiotic treatment is necessary at this time.
- Advised to use saline nasal spray as needed for any residual minor congestion.
- Patient to follow up on an as-needed basis.
- Discharged from current episode of care.

Electronically Signed,
Dr. Rohan Mehra
